---
title: "Troubleshooting"
output: html_document
---


# How to use mamba for package management in R?

```{bash}
# Install mini forge
wget https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge/releases/latest/download/Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh
bash Miniforge3-Linux-x86_64.sh

# Create new mamba environment
mamba create -n OSCA_Bioconductor r-base r-essentials

# Set the shell hook to correct directory
mamba shell init --shell zsh --root-prefix=~/miniforge3
source ~/.zshrc

# If the earlier step doesn't work check where the created enviroment is using the command below
mamba env list


```

# Issue faced and Solution (By Calude)

Great question. There were actually **two separate issues** that hit you in sequence:

## Issue 1: Shell not initialized
The first error said `Shell not initialized`. This happens because `mamba activate` isn't a regular command — it needs to hook into your shell's session to modify environment variables like `$PATH`. That hook has to be explicitly set up once per shell type. Since you're on zsh, it wasn't initialized, so mamba didn't know how to activate anything.

**Fix:** `eval "$(mamba shell hook --shell zsh)"` or `mamba shell init --shell zsh ...`

---

## Issue 2: Wrong root prefix
Once the shell was initialized, the second error said `prefix does not exist at ~/.local/share/mamba/envs/OSCA_Bioconductor`. This is because:

- Miniforge3 was already installed at `~/miniforge3/`, which is where your environments (including `OSCA_Bioconductor`) actually live
- But when you ran `mamba shell init`, you pointed it to `~/.local/share/mamba` as the root prefix — which was either the default suggested in the error message or a secondary/empty mamba installation
- So mamba was looking for the environment in the wrong place

**Fix:** Reinitialize with the correct root prefix — `~/miniforge3` — where your envs actually live.

---

## Summary

| Issue | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Shell not initialized | mamba's shell hook had never been set up for zsh | `mamba shell init --shell zsh` |
| Wrong root prefix | Initialized pointing to the wrong mamba installation | `--root-prefix=~/miniforge3` |

The zsh shell itself wasn't the root cause — the same issues would have occurred on bash. The real culprit was having miniforge3 already installed at a non-default location, and then initializing the shell hook pointing somewhere else.

> What is a shell hook? A shell hook = startup code that gives mamba permission to modify your shell's environment when you call activate.
